title: Ensure Resources are Closed Properly
description: Connections, streams, files, and other resources that implement the Closeable interface or its super-interface, AutoCloseable, must be closed after use. This close call must be made in a finally block otherwise an exception could prevent the call from being made. When a class implements AutoCloseable, the resource should ideally be created using the 'try-with-resources' pattern and will be closed automatically.
recommendation: Use the 'try-with-resources' pattern to ensure that each resource is closed at the end of the program, or ensure that 'close' is called in a finally block.
impact: Failure to properly close resources can result in a resource leak which could significantly degrade the performance of the application and potentially the machine it is running on.

noncompliant: private void readTheFile() throws IOException { Path path = Paths.get(this.fileName); BufferedReader reader = Files.newBufferedReader(path, this.charset); reader.close(); Files.lines("input.txt").forEach(System.out::println);}
private void doSomething() { OutputStream stream = null; try {for (String property : propertyList) {stream = new FileOutputStream("myfile.txt");}} catch (Exception e) {} finally {stream.close();}}
compliant: private void readTheFile(String fileName) throws IOException { Path path = Paths.get(fileName); try (BufferedReader reader = Files.newBufferedReader(path, StandardCharsets.UTF_8)) {reader.readLine();} try (Stream<String> input = Files.lines("input.txt"))  {input.forEach(System.out::println);}}
private void doSomething() { OutputStream stream = null; try {stream = new FileOutputStream("myfile.txt"); for (String property : propertyList) {}} catch (Exception e) {} finally {} }

checks.message: Use the 'try-with-resources' pattern to ensure that each resource is closed at the end of the program, or ensure that 'close' is called in a finally block.
